analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-332

POL-332.title: IMPLEMENT: CodeGraph integration analysis
POL-332.body:
## Objective

Execute the CodeGraph integration analysis for [POL-325](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-325/analyze-codegraph-integration-as-polaris-repository-intelligence-layer), producing a SmartDocs recommendation document that identifies which MIT-licensed CodeGraph components to selectively adopt into Polaris as a proprietary integrated capability.

## Context

Created by polaris-analyze from [POL-325](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-325/analyze-codegraph-integration-as-polaris-repository-intelligence-layer) (Analyze CodeGraph integration as Polaris repository intelligence layer). Strategic direction: selectively extract components from the MIT-licensed CodeGraph source and integrate them directly into the Polaris codebase — producing a proprietary combined result. Option A (direct dependency) and Option B (optional external backend) are off the table. The evaluation focuses on Option C (selective component adoption) vs Option D (clean reimplementation inspired by CodeGraph).

## Goal

Deliver a SmartDocs analysis document (`smartdocs/specs/active/`) that identifies the specific CodeGraph components worth extracting (graph model, SQLite layer, tree-sitter parsing), documents the MIT attribution requirements, and produces an integration roadmap for incorporating those components into `src/`.

## Scope

* smartdocs/specs/active/
* smartdocs/specs/raw/
* docs/Polaris/
* src/config/provider-detect.ts
* src/config/schema.ts
* src/map/
* src/cognition/
* src/loop/worker-prompt.ts
* src/loop/worker-packet.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* SmartDocs analysis document produced at `smartdocs/specs/active/pol-325-codegraph-integration-analysis.md`
* Document covers all five deliverable sections from [POL-325](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-325/analyze-codegraph-integration-as-polaris-repository-intelligence-layer): Repository Analysis, Capability Matrix, Integration Options (C vs D), Recommendation, and Governance
* MIT attribution requirements documented
* Specific components identified for extraction with target `src/` locations
* Recommendation includes a clear rationale and proposed integration roadmap
* All four child analysis tasks completed in dependency order

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

No dependency on other IMPLEMENT clusters. Can begin immediately.

## Non-goals

* No evaluation of Option A (direct CodeGraph dependency) or Option B (optional external backend provider)
* No production code changes in src/ during analysis
* No PRs or branch pushes during analysis phase

POL-336.title: Produce SmartDocs recommendation document for CodeGraph integration
POL-336.body:
## Objective

Synthesize findings from [POL-333](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-333/analyze-codegraph-source-architecture-and-data-model), [POL-334](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-334/map-polaris-repository-intelligence-capability-gaps-vs-codegraph), and [POL-335](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-335/evaluate-codegraph-integration-options-a-d-and-governance-implications) into a final SmartDocs recommendation document that delivers a per-component integration roadmap for bringing selected CodeGraph capabilities into the Polaris proprietary codebase.

## Context

Part of [POL-325](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-325/analyze-codegraph-integration-as-polaris-repository-intelligence-layer) (CodeGraph integration analysis). Terminal analysis child. Strategic direction confirmed: selective MIT component adoption (Option C) vs Polaris-native reimplementation (Option D), on a per-component basis. The combined result is proprietary. Inputs:

* `smartdocs/specs/raw/pol-325-codegraph-architecture.md` (from [POL-333](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-333/analyze-codegraph-source-architecture-and-data-model))
* `smartdocs/specs/raw/pol-325-capability-matrix.md` (from [POL-334](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-334/map-polaris-repository-intelligence-capability-gaps-vs-codegraph))
* `smartdocs/specs/raw/pol-325-integration-options.md` (from [POL-335](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-335/evaluate-codegraph-integration-options-a-d-and-governance-implications))

## Goal

Produce a final SmartDocs spec at `smartdocs/specs/active/pol-325-codegraph-integration-analysis.md` containing:

1. Executive summary and strategic direction (proprietary integration via selective MIT adoption)
2. CodeGraph architecture summary
3. Component extraction plan: per-component C vs D verdict with target `src/` locations
4. MIT attribution requirements: which notices to retain and where
5. Governance model for graph artifacts (storage, cognition exclusion, lifecycle)
6. Phased implementation roadmap with follow-on IMPLEMENT issues
7. Risks and open questions

Create follow-on IMPLEMENT issues for the actual integration work and link them from the document. Close [POL-325](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-325/analyze-codegraph-integration-as-polaris-repository-intelligence-layer) as complete.

## Scope

* smartdocs/specs/active/
* smartdocs/specs/raw/

## Acceptance Criteria

* SmartDocs document written to `smartdocs/specs/active/pol-325-codegraph-integration-analysis.md`
* Document has valid SmartDocs frontmatter (kind, status, source, created)
* Per-component integration plan present with C vs D verdicts
* MIT attribution section present and accurate
* Follow-on IMPLEMENT issues created and linked
* [POL-325](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-325/analyze-codegraph-integration-as-polaris-repository-intelligence-layer) closed as complete

## Validation

* npm run build

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-333](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-333/analyze-codegraph-source-architecture-and-data-model), [POL-334](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-334/map-polaris-repository-intelligence-capability-gaps-vs-codegraph), and [POL-335](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-335/evaluate-codegraph-integration-options-a-d-and-governance-implications).

## Non-goals

* No src/ changes
* No implementation of the integration
* No PR or branch push

POL-335.title: Evaluate CodeGraph integration options (A–D) and governance implications
POL-335.body:
## Objective

Evaluate Option C (selective component extraction) vs Option D (Polaris-native reimplementation inspired by CodeGraph) and produce a clear recommendation on which path to take for each candidate component.

## Context

Part of [POL-325](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-325/analyze-codegraph-integration-as-polaris-repository-intelligence-layer) (CodeGraph integration analysis). Depends on [POL-333](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-333/analyze-codegraph-source-architecture-and-data-model) (CodeGraph architecture) and [POL-334](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-334/map-polaris-repository-intelligence-capability-gaps-vs-codegraph) (capability matrix with extract/reimplement/skip verdicts). Strategic direction confirmed: MIT-licensed components will be incorporated directly into the Polaris proprietary codebase. Options A and B are out of scope. The question is whether to extract CodeGraph code directly (Option C) or use it as a reference to write Polaris-native equivalents (Option D), on a per-component basis.

## Goal

For each extraction candidate from [POL-334](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-334/map-polaris-repository-intelligence-capability-gaps-vs-codegraph), evaluate:

* **Option C**: lift the MIT source directly into `src/` (with attribution). Assess: code quality, coupling to CodeGraph internals, tree-sitter version compatibility, SQLite schema fit with Polaris atlas.
* **Option D**: reimplement the concept natively in Polaris TypeScript. Assess: effort, risk, divergence from a proven design.

Document MIT attribution requirements: the copyright notice must be retained in any source files taken from CodeGraph. The overall Polaris product remains proprietary — MIT does not require open-sourcing the combined work.

## Scope

* src/config/schema.ts
* src/config/provider-detect.ts
* src/config/defaults.ts
* polaris.config.json
* smartdocs/specs/raw/

## Acceptance Criteria

* Evaluation document written to `smartdocs/specs/raw/pol-325-integration-options.md`
* Option C vs D assessed per component (not as a single monolithic choice)
* MIT attribution obligations documented: what notices must be retained, in which files
* Governance section covers: graph artifact storage, exclusion from cognition ingestion, graph data lifecycle as a runtime artifact
* Tracker-agnostic architecture principle compliance confirmed for the chosen path

## Validation

* npm run build

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-333](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-333/analyze-codegraph-source-architecture-and-data-model) and [POL-334](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-334/map-polaris-repository-intelligence-capability-gaps-vs-codegraph).

## Non-goals

* Option A and Option B are explicitly out of scope — do not evaluate them
* No src/ code changes
* No implementation of any option

POL-334.title: Map Polaris repository intelligence capability gaps vs CodeGraph
POL-334.body:
## Objective

Compare CodeGraph capabilities against the current Polaris map/cognition/SmartDocs stack and identify which specific CodeGraph components are worth extracting for direct integration into Polaris.

## Context

Part of [POL-325](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-325/analyze-codegraph-integration-as-polaris-repository-intelligence-layer) (CodeGraph integration analysis). Depends on [POL-333](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-333/analyze-codegraph-source-architecture-and-data-model) (CodeGraph architecture findings). Strategic direction: selective MIT component adoption into a proprietary Polaris codebase — not an external dependency. This capability matrix should drive the component selection decision, not just document overlap.

## Goal

Produce a capability matrix and component extraction candidate list. For each CodeGraph subsystem identified in [POL-333](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-333/analyze-codegraph-source-architecture-and-data-model), assess: (1) what Polaris currently has, (2) what CodeGraph adds, (3) whether it's worth extracting or reimplementing. Primary candidates expected: graph data model (nodes/edges/symbols), SQLite persistence layer, tree-sitter parsing logic.

## Scope

* src/map/
* src/cognition/
* src/config/provider-detect.ts
* src/config/schema.ts
* src/loop/worker-prompt.ts
* src/loop/worker-packet.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/
* smartdocs/specs/raw/

## Acceptance Criteria

* Capability matrix written to `smartdocs/specs/raw/pol-325-capability-matrix.md`
* Matrix covers all four columns: Polaris atlas, GitNexus, SmartDocs, CodeGraph
* Each CodeGraph subsystem assessed with an explicit **extract / reimplement / skip** verdict and rationale
* MIT attribution obligations noted per component (which source files carry the notice)
* Gap analysis focused on worker context and token efficiency improvements
* Proposed target `src/` locations for each extraction candidate

## Validation

* npm run build

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-333](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-333/analyze-codegraph-source-architecture-and-data-model).

## Non-goals

* No evaluation of keeping CodeGraph as an external runtime dependency
* No recommendation (that is [POL-336](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-336/produce-smartdocs-recommendation-document-for-codegraph-integration)'s responsibility)
* No src/ changes

POL-333.title: Analyze CodeGraph source architecture and data model
POL-333.body:
## Objective

Read and document the internal architecture, subsystems, and data model of the CodeGraph project from its MIT-licensed source.

## Context

Part of [POL-325](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-325/analyze-codegraph-integration-as-polaris-repository-intelligence-layer) (CodeGraph integration analysis). This is the foundational research child — subsequent children depend on this output. CodeGraph source: [https://github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph/tree/main](<https://github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph/tree/main>)

## Goal

Produce a structured findings document covering: major subsystems, tree-sitter parsing approach, symbol extraction model, graph node/edge schema, SQLite storage model, relationship resolution, MCP integration surface, and indexing strategy. Document should be sufficient for downstream capability comparison and integration option evaluation.

## Scope

* smartdocs/specs/raw/
* docs/Polaris/

## Acceptance Criteria

* Architecture overview document written to `smartdocs/specs/raw/pol-325-codegraph-architecture.md`
* Document covers: subsystems, data model (nodes, edges, symbols), storage (SQLite schema), indexing strategy, MCP integration surface
* Tree-sitter parser coverage (languages/symbol types) documented
* Local-first operation model and runtime dependencies documented

## Validation

* npm run build

## Ordering

None — this child has no dependencies and executes first.

## Non-goals

* No evaluation of integration options
* No Polaris capability comparison
* No src/ changes

POL-325.title: Analyze CodeGraph integration as Polaris repository intelligence layer
